app.name: Wasabi
app.subtitle: Workflow for Systematic and Bibliometric Integrated Analysis

home.description: In Wasabi, you record the review protocol, import and organize Scopus data, and analyze the corpus through bibliometric indicators, charts, and networks. Methodological decisions remain together in the same project, from the search to the final synthesis.
home.guideDescription: A literature review project can be prepared in a few steps:

create.title: Create the project
create.description: Enter the question, scope, searched databases, and review criteria.

import.title: Import the corpus
import.description: Add the CSV file exported by Scopus and check the field mapping.

review.title: Review the results
review.description: Examine duplicates, indicators, charts, networks, and clusters.

export.title: Keep a copy
export.description: Export the project as .wasabi.json and generate review tables or the report.

home.temacDescription: Wasabi follows the three TEMAC stages: research preparation; data presentation and interrelation; and evidence detailing and validation.

sample.title: Example: integrated scientific mapping
sample.question: How do bibliometric methods support evidence synthesis?
sample.inclusion: Articles and full papers on bibliometric analysis
sample.notes: Fictitious project created only to demonstrate the features.

stage.preparationDescription: Define objectives, search strategy, scope, and criteria.
stage.interrelationDescription: Import, normalize, and explore corpus indicators.
stage.evidenceDescription: Build networks, clusters, and the review synthesis.

privacy.localProcessing: Your data is processed locally in this browser. To back up or share your project, export the .wasabi.json file.
privacy.detail: Imported files are not sent to external servers. Reading and analysis take place in this browser.

project.formDescription: Record the initial methodological decisions. Enter list items one per line.
project.listDescription: Projects stored locally in this browser.
project.empty: No saved projects. Create your first project to start the TEMAC workflow.
project.confirmDelete: Delete the project “{{title}}”? This action cannot be undone.

period.title: Research period
period.automaticHelp: The earliest and latest years found in the Scopus file will be recorded when the corpus is added.
period.manualHelp: Enter the interval defined in the research protocol.

identification.title: Identification and purpose
identification.description: Present the topic, purpose, and question guiding the review.

search.title: Search strategy
search.description: Record how documents were retrieved from Scopus. Copy the query exactly as it was executed.

selection.title: Scope and study selection
selection.description: Document the rules used to decide which records belong in the review. These rules are methodological decisions, not fields extracted automatically from the CSV.

notes.title: Methodological record
notes.description: Record decisions, adjustments, and circumstances another person would need to understand or reproduce the research.

help.title: Enter a clear and specific title that identifies the topic and purpose of the research or review.
help.objective: State what the research intends to analyze, compare, map, or understand.
help.researchQuestion: Write the question that will be answered from the corpus. It should delimit the object, context, or relationship under study.
help.descriptors: Terms and expressions representing the topic and used to build the search. Enter one per line.
help.searchStrings: Paste the search expression exactly as executed in Scopus, including AND/OR operators, quotation marks, parentheses, and fields such as TITLE-ABS-KEY.
help.databases: The database where the search was conducted. Use Scopus in this version.
help.knowledgeAreas: Areas defined in the protocol or used as Scopus filters. The supplied CSV has no subject-area column, so the researcher completes this field.
help.inclusionCriteria: Rules for retaining a study after retrieval, such as relevance to the topic, document type, language, period, population, or full-text availability. They may repeat Scopus filters but should also record screening decisions.
help.exclusionCriteria: Rules for removing a study from the corpus, such as being outside the scope, being an editorial, lacking essential information, or not addressing the question. They are not merely options selected during CSV export.
help.methodologicalNotes: Record changes to the strategy, search date, filters, limitations, screening decisions, and other important information.

examples.title: Example: Scientific mapping of digital education research
examples.objective: Example: Analyze the evolution, leading authors, and thematic fronts of digital education.
examples.researchQuestion: Example: How did digital education research evolve between 2015 and 2025?
examples.descriptors:
digital education
online learning
educational technology
examples.inclusionCriteria:
Articles and full papers related to the question
Documents published within the defined period
Languages accepted by the review
examples.exclusionCriteria:
Documents unrelated to the topic
Editorials, errata, or abstracts without full text
Records missing essential information
examples.methodologicalNotes: Example: Search conducted on 17 July 2026. Language and document-type filters were applied in Scopus.

import.dropTitle: Select the CSV file exported by Scopus
import.formats: Scopus CSV file — read only in this browser
import.parseWarnings: The import produced {{count}} warning(s). You may continue and review the preview.
import.detectedPeriod: Period found in the file: {{start}}–{{end}}. These years will be recorded in the project when the corpus is added.

scopusHelp.title: Need help? See how to obtain the CSV from Scopus
scopusHelp.intro: Sign in to Scopus with an account that has Elsevier access, run the search, and open the results list.
scopusHelp.step1: Select the desired results, open Export, and choose CSV format.
scopusHelp.step1Alt: Scopus Export menu showing the CSV option
scopusHelp.step2: In the export window, use Select all information or select the required groups, then click Export.
scopusHelp.step2Alt: Scopus window for selecting CSV export fields
scopusHelp.fields: For more complete analyses, include citation information, bibliographic data, abstract and keywords, affiliations, DOI, and references.
scopusHelp.onlyScopus: This Wasabi version works only with Scopus CSV files. Other databases will be included in later versions.

duplicates.title: Review {{count}} duplicate group(s)
duplicates.description: The most complete record was selected automatically. If needed, choose a different record to retain in the corpus.

dashboard.title: Bibliometric dashboard
dashboard.description: Indicators calculated locally from the deduplicated records.
dashboard.sankey: Flow across year, source, and document type

networks.title: Bibliometric Maps
networks.description: Build networks, detect communities, and explore corpus relationships.
networks.performanceWarning: This corpus has more than 100 documents. Co-citation networks may take a few seconds; use minimum weight and Top N to simplify the view.
networks.emptyCorpus: Import records in the interrelation stage before building networks.

typeHelp.coauthorship: Connects authors who published together. Link strength shows how many works they shared.
typeHelp.keyword-cooccurrence: Connects keywords found in the same documents and reveals related topics.
typeHelp.bibliographic-coupling: Connects documents that cite the same references; greater overlap suggests stronger thematic proximity.
typeHelp.cocitation: Connects references cited together by corpus documents and reveals the field's intellectual base.
typeHelp.citation: Shows citations found among the imported documents; arrows indicate citation direction.

modeHelp.network: Displays nodes, links, and communities in one interactive map.
modeHelp.cluster: Uses color to highlight communities detected automatically from link proximity.
modeHelp.density: Shows where item concentration and weight are highest: blue is lower; yellow and red mark more intense areas.
modeHelp.overlay: Colors nodes by a selected measure to compare position and intensity.

overlayHelp.weight: Frequency or occurrence of the item in the network.
overlayHelp.citations: Number of citations received by the document.
overlayHelp.degree: Number of direct links held by the node.
overlayHelp.weightedDegree: Sum of the strength of all links held by the node.
overlayHelp.betweenness: How often the node acts as a bridge between different parts of the network.
overlayHelp.year: Year associated with the document; colors help reveal temporal evolution.

controlHelp.minimumWeight: Hides links below this value. Raise it to reduce noise; lower it to see weaker relationships.
controlHelp.topN: Limits the view to the highest-weight items. A smaller number improves legibility without changing the corpus.

node.description: Each circle represents an author, keyword, document, or reference, depending on the selected network.

edge.description: A line records a relationship between two nodes. Thicker lines represent stronger relationships.

weight.description: Measures frequency or strength. It affects node size and link thickness.

cluster.description: A group of nodes more closely connected to one another. Colors separate thematic or relational communities.

networks.interactionHelp: Hover over a node to highlight its relationships. Click for details, drag nodes or the background, and use the mouse wheel to zoom.
networks.selectNode: Select a node to inspect its data.

exports.title: Export network map and data
exports.description: Use SVG for editing and vector publication, or high-resolution PNG (3200 px) for presentations and documents.

evidence.title: Visualization notes and evidence
evidence.description: The summary below follows the displayed network, mode, and filters. Notes are saved when the field loses focus.
evidence.visualizationPlaceholder: Record what this map, mode, or overlay reveals…
evidence.label: Suggested label
evidence.placeholder: Record the theme, evidence, and relationships observed…

export.dataDescription: Download the deduplicated corpus and main bibliometric tables.
export.reportDescription: Generate the final report in the desired language and format.
export.synthesisPlaceholder: Write your interpretation of the results and evidence…

report.generatedWith: Analyses generated locally with Wasabi on {{date}}.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Adjust the interface reading experience and manage data stored in this browser.
settings.dataDescription: Export a copy before clearing projects from this browser.
settings.confirmClear: Delete every project stored in this browser? Export a backup before continuing.

errors.saveProject: The project could not be saved.
errors.invalidProject: The file is not a valid Wasabi project.
errors.scopusCsvOnly: Select a CSV file exported by Scopus.

common.comingSoon: Available in the next MVP stages
